sourceMovie.title: Edge of Darkness
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Edge of Darkness for the same reason it broke through in 2010: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Martin Campbell keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Edge of Darkness clicked for you, A Walk Among the Tombstones (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Edge of Darkness?
faq.answer: Try A Walk Among the Tombstones, Arlington Road, In the Valley of Elah—each shares DNA with Edge of Darkness while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Edge of Darkness?
faq.answer: Edge of Darkness (2010) is best described as Crime, Drama, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Edge of Darkness worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
